name: Billie Holiday
abstract: Billie Holiday (born Eleanora Fagan; April 7, 1915 – July 17, 1959) was an American jazz and swing music singer. Nicknamed "Lady Day" by her friend and music partner, Lester Young, Holiday made significant contributions to jazz music and pop singing.

works_created.t: Tell me more and more (and then some)

about_quotes.speaker: Jerry Bergonzi
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] I used to think that — imagine being a piano player with like Billie Holiday and making this judgement well do I go with her? Do I play where she’s at or do I just like straight ahead? [INTERVIEWEE] Yeah.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jerry Bergonzi interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, June 7, 2022

about_quotes.speaker: Chico Freeman
about_quotes.evidence: You look at people like Monk, Charlie Parker, Duke Ellington, Count Basie, Louis Armstrong, Billie Holiday. When you look at these people, they have one thing in common and that is they were all original.
about_quotes.doc_title: Chico Freeman interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 13, 2001

about_quotes.speaker: Lew Soloff
about_quotes.evidence: Billie Holiday’s voice on “Lady in Satin,” was, not really so great anymore, and yet it’s the most moving record that I’ve ever heard by a singer, to me.
about_quotes.doc_title: Lew Soloff interviewed by Monk Rowe, Scottsdale, Arizona, April 16, 2000

about_quotes.speaker: Jimmy Wormworth
about_quotes.evidence: In the thirties in Utica my father played with Billie Holiday and Helen Humes. I know because I worked with Helen Humes later and she told me about it.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jimmy Wormworth interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 6, 2002

about_quotes.speaker: Jimmy Owens
about_quotes.evidence: The music that my father played most of the time were people like Duke Ellington, Billie Holliday... this is what I was hearing when I was a kid.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jimmy Owens — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Wendy Oxenhorn
about_quotes.evidence: I would get on the swings and I would hear Bennie Moten, and the Kansas City Five, and Billie Holiday, and I remember thinking that was my music.
about_quotes.doc_title: Wendy Oxenhorn — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
